sections.title: 02a — Underlying mechanism
sections.content:
Turnkey systems integration of (containerized Li-ion BESS + bidirectional PCS + MV grid coupling + EMS dispatch + civil works) delivered under a single EPC contract with VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 certification. **Mechanism is the execution methodology (single-source integration), not a physical artifact** — consistent with Project Business offering-type.

Physical principle of the delivered asset: reversible lithium intercalation in LFP cells, 4-quadrant power electronics, MV transformation. These are components inside the deliverable, not the deliverable itself.

---

sections.title: 02b — Product FP (competitive positioning)
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `statement` | Deliver grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy storage as a turnkey capital asset with single-source responsibility from site study to commissioning |
| `verb` | Deliver |
| `object` | grid-compliant, dispatchable multi-MWh electrical energy storage |
| `context` | as a turnkey capital asset with single-source responsibility from site study to commissioning |
| `differentiators` | ① Single-point EPC accountability vs. split-contract; ② Integrated PV+BESS design authority from PV-EPC incumbency; ③ Native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 path; ④ Proven industrial scale (4.5 MW / 9 MWh reference) |

**Mechanism-free test:** PASS. Statement makes no reference to lithium, LFP, inverters, or any physical principle — swap the storage medium (e.g., flow battery, CAES) and the promise still holds for the EPC delivery layer.

---

sections.title: 02c — UNSPSC classification (recap from 01d)
sections.content:
No 8-digit commodity reaches ≥80% confidence. Custom product group used.

| | Value |
|---|---|
| `unspsc_code` | `null` |
| `custom_product_group` | **Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services** |

Top evaluated: 81101513 Electrical engineering (55%), 72151500 Specialized trade construction (50%), 26111711 Lithium batteries (45%). All cover sub-scopes, none cover the bundled EPC deliverable.

---

sections.title: 02d — Commodity FP (drives market discovery)
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `commodity_or_group` | Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services |
| `functional_promise` | **Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification** |
| `verb` | Deliver |
| `object` | operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets |
| `context` | as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification |
| `reasoning` | All BESS EPC providers (pure-play integrators, utility IPPs, solar EPCs extending into storage, OEM-led turnkey offers) share this function: converting a storage business case into a commissioned, grid-compliant asset. Mechanism varies, function does not. |

**Vendor-agnostic test:** PASS. No brand/product names, no Mein Solar-specific differentiators.

---

sections.title: 02e — FP Extension (broader-than-commodity capability)
sections.content:
Mein Solar's underlying EPC capability is **broader than BESS**. Two orthogonal extension axes:

### Axis 1 — Storage technology neutrality (same function, different medium)
The Commodity FP "deliver grid-connected electrical energy storage" applies to any storage medium that presents a grid-facing AC interface under VDE-AR-N 4110.

| Extension domain | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Flow-battery storage EPC (vanadium, iron-salt) | Same PCS / MV / EMS / civil / cert path; different cell stack |
| Mechanical storage EPC (flywheel, CAES small-scale) | Same grid-coupling + cert scope; different prime mover |
| Thermal-to-power storage EPC (hot-rock, molten-salt-TES with ORC) | Same grid interface, different charge/discharge block |
| Hybrid H2-BESS EPC | Same single-source-integration promise across electrolyzer + storage + fuel-cell |

### Axis 2 — Grid-connected capital-asset EPC methodology (same vendor execution, different asset)
The single-source-EPC + VDE-AR-N 4110 + FGW TR8 capability transfers to any grid-connected power asset, not only storage.

| Extension domain | Rationale |
|---|---|
| Large-scale PV EPC | Mein Solar's incumbent business; same grid-code regime |
| High-power EV charging park EPC (HPC hubs) | Same MV coupling, civil, permit, DSO interface — buffered by BESS |
| Co-located PV+Wind hybrid parks | Same grid-code certification methodology |
| Industrial microgrid / Inselnetz EPC | Adds islanding controls but same integration core |
| Behind-the-meter industrial electrification EPC (heat-pump + BESS + PV) | Same single-source wrapper, broader asset mix |

### FP Extension statement

> The underlying EPC execution capability — single-source integration, VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 certification delivery, and MV grid coupling — applies beyond BESS to **any grid-connected electrical capital asset where an owner wants one counterparty to convert a business case into a commissioned, certified, operational asset**.

**Phase A secondary search** will run against this extension to surface NAICS markets that need grid-connected capital-asset EPC but not specifically BESS (e.g., HPC hubs, industrial microgrids).

---

sections.title: 02f — VN / BOM position
sections.content:
### Value-network position

| Tier | Role | Mein Solar? |
|---|---|---|
| L3 | Cell / module makers (CATL, EVE, BYD) | — |
| L2 | Container / stack integrators (Sungrow PowerTitan, Tesla Megapack, CATL EnerC, SolarEast) | — |
| L2 | PCS makers (SMA, Sungrow, Power Electronics, Ingeteam) | — |
| L2 | MV equipment (Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton) | — |
| **L1** | **BESS EPC / system integrator** | **✓ Mein Solar sits here** |
| L0 | Asset owner / IPP / C&I offtaker | Customer |

### BOM position

Mein Solar delivers the **complete L0 asset** (the commissioned, certified storage plant) by assembling L2 sub-systems. The product IS the L0-level deliverable — not a component inside something larger.

### FP context implication
A **system-level FP** (broad: "deliver a storage asset") is correct here. A component-level FP ("convert DC to AC", "store charge") would understate what Mein Solar sells. Market discovery must search for markets needing the system-level outcome, not the sub-component function.

---

sections.title: 02g — Required complements
sections.content:
Needed to deliver on the Product FP. Classified by criticality.

| Complement | Criticality | Type | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| MV grid connection capacity (DSO) | **Essential** | Infrastructure | Hard blocker; grid upgrade cost can invalidate project |
| Buildable site (owned or leased) with Baugenehmigung | **Essential** | Real estate / regulatory | 40–80 m²/MWh; fire clearances; Feuerwehr access |
| Cell / container supply (L2 vendor) | **Essential** | Equipment | LFP container from CATL / Sungrow / SolarEast / Tesla |
| PCS + MV switchgear supply | **Essential** | Equipment | Siemens / ABB / SMA / Sungrow / Power Electronics |
| DSO grid-code certification body | **Essential** | Regulatory | TÜV / FGW-accredited certifier; 6–12 months lead |
| Project financing / asset-owner capex | **Essential** | Capital | €250–400/kWh installed; bank or investor |
| O&M provider (post-COD) | **Essential** | Service | Either Mein Solar or third party; warranty + availability SLA |
| Insurance (fire, performance, business interruption) | **Essential** | Risk | Specialty lines; drives fire-protection scope |
| Revenue-stream access: peak-shaving load, arbitrage market (EPEX), ancillary-services prequal (FCR/aFRR), §19 StromNEV | **Essential** | Market access | Without a revenue stream the asset has no business case |
| EMS / VPP / aggregator integration | Enhancing | Software | Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Flexpower, Enspired, Tiko |
| Co-located PV generator | Enhancing | Equipment | Unlocks the "Integrated PV+BESS" differentiator |
| HPC charging dispensers | Enhancing | Equipment | Unlocks the "EV-charging buffer" differentiator |
| Cloud monitoring / asset-performance platform | Enhancing | Software | Visibility + SLA evidence |
| Cybersecurity / IEC 62443 compliance layer | Enhancing | Regulatory | Required for utility-facing ancillary-service participation |
| Intraday / balancing market trading desk | Optional | Service | Via aggregator if owner lacks in-house |
| Second-life / recycling partner (EU Battery Reg.) | Optional | Compliance | End-of-life obligation under CRMA / EU Battery Reg. |
| Black-start / islanding capability | Optional | Feature | Only needed for microgrid use-cases |

---

sections.title: 02h — QA checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Product FP present (statement/verb/object/context/diff.) | ✅ |
| 2 | Product FP passes mechanism-free test | ✅ |
| 3 | Commodity FP vendor-agnostic | ✅ |
| 4 | UNSPSC code or custom product group identified | ✅ (custom group; UNSPSC below 80%) |
| 5 | VN / BOM position mapped | ✅ (L1 EPC integrator; delivers L0 asset) |
| 6 | Required complements listed with criticality | ✅ |
| 7 | Source tags on factual claims | ✅ (sources in §01 upstream) |
| 8 | FP Extension assessed | ✅ (two axes: storage-medium neutrality + grid-asset EPC methodology) |

---

sections.title: 02i — Neo4j write preview
sections.content:
```
(NeutralProductGroup {name:"Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services"})
   -[:has_functional_promise]-> (FunctionalPromise {scope:"commodity", ...})
   -[:has_fp_extension]-> (FPExtension {axis:"storage_medium"})
   -[:has_fp_extension]-> (FPExtension {axis:"grid_asset_epc_methodology"})
(Product {name:"EPC für Großbatteriespeicher", company_name:"Mein Solar GmbH"})
   -[:has_functional_promise]-> (FunctionalPromise {scope:"product", ...})
   -[:requires_complement]-> (Complement) x N   (9 essential, 5 enhancing, 3 optional)
   -[:positioned_at]-> (ValueNetworkTier {level:"L1_EPC_Integrator"})
```

---
